channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Wafer-scale high-κ HfO<sub>2</sub> dielectric films with sub-5-Å equivalent oxide thickness for 2D MoS<sub>2</sub> transistors
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 20 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-68584-0

The integration of high-κ dielectrics with low equivalent oxide thickness (EOT) is crucial for the development of 2D transistors. Here, the authors report the low-temperature fabrication of wafer-scale HfO2 dielectric films with sub-5-Å EOT and their application for the realization of high-performance 2D MoS2 transistors and circuits.
item.dc:title: Wafer-scale high-κ HfO<sub>2</sub> dielectric films with sub-5-Å equivalent oxide thickness for 2D MoS<sub>2</sub> transistors
item.dc:creator: Songge Zhang

item.title: Intrinsically stretchable 2D MoS<sub>2</sub> transistors
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 20 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-68504-2

Intrinsically stretchable electronic devices are interesting for wearable electronics, soft robotics, and stretchable display applications. Here, the authors report the fabrication of intrinsically stretchable solution-processed thin-film transistors based on 2D MoS2 flakes, showing high performance and stability under strain up to 20%.
item.dc:title: Intrinsically stretchable 2D MoS<sub>2</sub> transistors
item.dc:creator: Kyunghun Kim
